items.description: Identify all shared services requiring TSA coverage at close
items.mustHaveReason: Day 1 TSA identification is foundational — all other 69 TSA items depend on it. Removal creates immediate service continuity gap at close.

items.description: Define SLA metrics for each TSA service category
Establish TSA pricing model (cost-plus baseline)
Map TSA exit milestones by service category
items.mustHaveReason: TSA exit milestone mapping drives integration timeline. Programs without this item average 47 more days of TSA dependency.

items.description: Assess standalone capability for each TSA service
items.mustHaveReason: Standalone capability assessment is the prerequisite for all exit planning. Removing blocks TSA termination readiness.

items.description: Establish TSA governance committee and escalation path
Document TSA invoice reconciliation process
Identify all IT infrastructure under TSA scope
items.mustHaveReason: IT infrastructure under TSA must be scoped on Day 1 — affects all system access, email, and identity for the acquired workforce.

items.description: Define email and identity transition timeline under TSA
items.mustHaveReason: Email and identity transition is an operational Day 1 dependency for the entire acquired workforce. Cannot be deferred.

items.description: Identify finance systems covered under TSA (ERP, payroll, reporting)
items.mustHaveReason: Finance system TSA coverage must be confirmed at close for financial reporting continuity and first-close integrity.

items.description: Establish interim financial reporting process under TSA
Identify stranded costs post-TSA exit by service
Identify cross-border TSA services and regulatory implications
Build TSA exit roadmap with service dependency sequencing
items.mustHaveReason: TSA exit roadmap with service dependency sequencing is required to manage interdependencies across workstreams.

items.description: Identify HR services requiring TSA coverage (payroll, benefits, HRIS)
items.mustHaveReason: HR services (payroll, benefits, HRIS) under TSA must be identified on Day 1 — impacts all employees immediately.

items.description: Define TSA scope for employee benefits administration and carrier contracts
Establish interim HRIS access and data transfer protocols under TSA
Map HR TSA exit dependencies on standalone HRIS implementation
Assess cross-border HR TSA implications for international employee populations
Document TSA exit plan for payroll processing capability transfer
Validate standalone HR operations readiness before TSA termination
Identify legal services and entities requiring TSA support post-close
Define TSA scope for corporate secretarial and entity management services
Establish interim IP and contract management process under TSA
Map litigation support services covered under TSA and handover timelines
Assess standalone legal team hiring or outside counsel retention for TSA exit
Confirm compliance obligations covered under legal TSA with regulatory calendar
Identify procurement and sourcing services requiring TSA coverage at close
Define TSA scope for vendor management and preferred supplier agreements
Establish interim purchase order approval workflow under TSA
Assess strategic sourcing capability gaps requiring extended TSA coverage
Map cross-border procurement TSA to applicable trade compliance requirements
Build standalone procurement organization transition plan for TSA exit
Design TSA performance scorecard with KPIs for each service tower
Implement monthly TSA SLA compliance reporting cadence
Establish service credit mechanism for SLA breaches under TSA
Conduct quarterly TSA service quality reviews with provider and buyer stakeholders
Assess TSA service degradation risk as provider begins separation activities
Benchmark TSA service costs versus market rates for renewal or renegotiation decisions
Define TSA dispute resolution process and escalation tiers
Establish TSA invoice dispute log and resolution SLA
Document criteria for referring TSA disputes to independent arbitration
Confirm cross-border TSA dispute jurisdiction and governing law clauses
Track open TSA disputes and financial exposure in governance reporting
Conduct TSA transition readiness assessment for each service tower
items.mustHaveReason: TSA transition readiness assessment is the gating item for exit authorization. Removing creates uncontrolled exit risk.

items.description: Develop go/no-go criteria for TSA exit authorization by service
items.mustHaveReason: Go/no-go criteria for TSA exit prevents premature service termination. Programs without this item experience 2.3× more service failures.

items.description: Perform dry-run cutover test for top three TSA services before exit
Validate data migration completeness prior to TSA system service termination
items.mustHaveReason: Data migration completeness must be validated before TSA system termination. Data loss risk is irreversible.

items.description: Confirm employee training completion for standalone tools replacing TSA services
Execute formal TSA service termination notice per contractual notice periods
items.mustHaveReason: Formal termination notice must be executed within contractual notice periods. Failure creates ongoing TSA obligations and cost.

items.description: Complete post-exit stability review 30 days after each TSA service termination
Establish cost allocation methodology for shared TSA services with multiple consumers
Map TSA costs to functional budget owners for accountability tracking
Validate TSA cost allocation treatment for management and statutory reporting
Assess transfer pricing implications of cross-border TSA cost allocations
Implement monthly TSA cost allocation reporting for finance consolidation
Reconcile TSA cost allocations to provider invoices on a monthly basis
Quantify total TSA cost run rate and compare against standalone cost model
Define TSA extension triggers and approval thresholds for delayed exits
Establish TSA extension pricing uplift schedule to incentivize timely exit
Review TSA extension requests against transition readiness assessment findings
Obtain governance committee approval for any TSA extensions beyond original term
Model financial impact of TSA extensions on stranded cost projections
Track and report TSA extension history for post-deal integration lessons learned
Build stranded cost model for each TSA service post-exit by functional area
Develop mitigation plan to absorb or eliminate stranded costs within 12 months
Assess data residency requirements for cross-border TSA data flows
Confirm VAT and indirect tax treatment of cross-border TSA service fees
Obtain local regulatory approvals required before terminating cross-border TSA services
Conduct final TSA close-out review and archive all TSA documentation
Map target COA to acquirer COA structure (accounting-layer mapping for consolidation)
items.mustHaveReason: First combined financial close process must be defined before Day 1 — delayed setup causes material misstatement risk.

items.description: Identify intercompany elimination entries
items.mustHaveReason: Chart of accounts mapping is required for any consolidated reporting. Blocking item for all finance workstreams.

items.description: Configure consolidation journal entries
items.mustHaveReason: Intercompany elimination setup is required for first consolidated close. Cannot be retroactively applied.

items.description: Assess GAAP conversion requirements (if target GAAP differs)
Document differences between target and acquirer accounting policies
Prepare first post-close consolidated financial statements
Identify statutory reporting obligations for all entities
File change-of-control statutory notifications
Establish consolidated reporting calendar and close schedule
Establish intercompany billing and reconciliation process
Engage valuation firm to perform purchase price allocation (PPA) under ASC 805
Identify and fair-value all acquired intangible assets (customer lists, IP, trademarks)
Record preliminary opening balance sheet adjustments for fair-value step-ups
Finalize PPA within the ASC 805 measurement period (12 months from close)
Establish amortization schedules for all identified intangible assets post-PPA
Calculate initial goodwill arising from acquisition and record at close
Assign goodwill to reporting units in accordance with ASC 350
Perform qualitative goodwill impairment assessment (Step 0) at year-end
Conduct quantitative goodwill impairment test if qualitative triggers are identified
Document indefinite-lived intangible impairment testing methodology and results
Assess impact of acquisition on reportable segment structure under ASC 280
Redefine operating segment boundaries incorporating acquired business units
Map target cost centre and P&L structure to acquirer segment taxonomy
Prepare first combined segment footnote disclosures for external filing
Align budget and forecast templates across acquirer and target for first full planning cycle
Establish combined-entity flash reporting cadence (weekly/monthly) for executive team
Produce first integrated monthly management accounts with variance commentary
items.mustHaveReason: Purchase price allocation must be completed within reporting deadlines. Missing this item creates SEC/GAAP compliance risk.

items.description: Define and agree synergy tracking methodology and reporting format with leadership
items.mustHaveReason: Opening balance sheet must be prepared at close. Deferring creates cascading restatement risk.

items.description: Brief external audit partner on acquisition scope, PPA timeline, and key risks
Agree audit approach and procedures for acquisition-related accounting estimates
Provide auditors access to target dataroom, financial records, and valuation models
Resolve all open pre-close audit adjustments from target prior-year audit
Coordinate combined-entity year-end audit timeline and deliverable schedule
Determine SEC filing obligations triggered by acquisition (8-K, S-4, proxy)
File Form 8-K with audited target financial statements within 71-day deadline
Prepare pro forma combined financial statements for SEC 8-K/A filing
Update 10-Q/10-K disclosures to reflect acquisition accounting and risk factors
Confirm MD&A narrative covers acquisition impact on results of operations
Identify functional currency of all acquired legal entities under ASC 830
Configure consolidation system with correct translation rates (closing, average, historical) per entity
Establish FX hedging and exposure reporting for newly acquired foreign currency cash flows
Validate cumulative translation adjustment (CTA) balance carried over at close
Obtain complete lease population from target and validate against GL balances
Re-measure acquired lease liabilities and right-of-use assets at acquisition date under ASC 842
Migrate target lease data into acquirer lease management system (e.g., CoStar, LeaseQuery)
Review and reclassify any operating vs. finance lease classifications for acquired portfolio
Review target revenue recognition policies and assess alignment with acquirer ASC 606 methodology
Document five-step revenue recognition model for all acquired contract types
Identify acquired deferred revenue balances subject to fair-value haircut under ASC 805
Assess whether existing consolidation platform (e.g., OneStream, HFM) can support combined entity structure
Configure acquirer consolidation system with new entity hierarchy, intercompany rules, and FX settings for acquired entities
Establish earnout / contingent consideration tracking and ASC 805 remeasurement process
Execute working capital true-up per SPA — validate peg, calculate adjustment, meet contractual deadline
Assess inherited defined benefit pension and OPEB obligations under ASC 715
Determine ASC 718 treatment of acquired equity awards (replacement, acceleration, forfeiture)
Calculate modification charge and ongoing expense for converted stock-based awards
Validate AP cutoff procedures at close
Establish intercompany billing process
Execute bank account cutover at close
Update signatories on all target bank accounts
Confirm payroll continuity for all employees on close date
Establish payroll accrual methodology post-close
Review and approve all open purchase orders at close
Review open AR aging and agree collection process
Obtain and reconcile target fixed asset register to close balance sheet
Assign acquirer asset categories and useful life policies to all target fixed assets
Record purchase price allocation step-up adjustments to fixed assets in ERP
Conduct physical asset verification for all major asset classes at target sites
Integrate target capex approval workflow with acquirer purchase authorization policy
Validate inventory count and valuation methodology at close date
Align inventory costing method (FIFO, LIFO, weighted average) to acquirer policy
Establish obsolescence reserve methodology consistent with acquirer standards
Reconcile raw material, WIP, and finished goods subledger to general ledger
Migrate inventory item master to acquirer ERP with validated unit costs
Compile complete lease schedule for all target real estate and equipment leases
Classify all leases under ASC 842 (or IFRS 16) using acquirer discount rates
Record right-of-use assets and lease liabilities in acquirer general ledger
Identify leases with change-of-control clauses requiring landlord or lessor consent
Load target lease data into acquirer lease management system
Document target revenue recognition policies and compare to acquirer ASC 606 methodology
Identify performance obligations in target customer contracts requiring reallocation
Align contract modification and variable consideration policies to acquirer standards
Migrate target deferred revenue balances and validate to source billing records
Establish combined-entity revenue close process including SSP documentation
Document target standard cost system and compare to acquirer costing methodology
Align overhead allocation rates and cost center hierarchy to acquirer structure
Identify and eliminate intercompany profit in inventory at close
Establish variance analysis reporting for material, labor, and overhead post-close
Run parallel cost rolls in both legacy and acquirer cost systems for one full cycle
Stand up target entity in acquirer general ledger with validated opening balances
Define journal entry approval thresholds and workflow for combined entity
Establish recurring journal entry templates for all significant accruals
Migrate historical GL data for prior periods required by financial reporting
Configure automated subledger-to-GL reconciliation for all major modules
Define account reconciliation policy including frequency, ownership, and sign-off requirements
Assign reconciliation ownership for all balance sheet accounts in combined entity
Complete first full balance sheet reconciliation pack for combined entity
Implement reconciliation tool or template standardization across acquired entities
Document target month-end close calendar and compare to acquirer close schedule
Establish unified close calendar harmonizing target and acquirer deadline cadences
Identify close bottlenecks in acquired entity and assign remediation owners
Execute first integrated month-end close under combined entity structure
Conduct post-close retrospective and update process for cycle-two improvements
Identify all intercompany transaction flows between acquirer and target entities
items.mustHaveReason: Legal entity tax registration changes must be filed within statutory deadlines. Late filing triggers penalties.

items.description: Establish intercompany transfer pricing policy for all material transaction types
items.mustHaveReason: Consolidated tax group election timing is irrevocable in many jurisdictions. Decision cannot be deferred.

items.description: Configure intercompany netting and settlement process in ERP for month-end
Validate intercompany elimination entries for first consolidated close
Review target credit policy and align credit limit approval authority to acquirer standards
Assess target bad debt reserve adequacy and align to acquirer provisioning methodology
Notify customers of change-of-control and provide updated remittance instructions
Integrate target collections workflow into acquirer AR management platform
Communicate acquirer T&E policy to all acquired entity employees at close
Migrate target employees into acquirer expense management platform
Reconcile outstanding T&E expense reports filed under legacy target system
Map target vendor master to acquirer vendor master and identify duplicates
Rationalize overlapping vendor relationships and consolidate for pricing leverage
Transition target procurement approvals to acquirer purchase order workflow
Identify and renegotiate target contracts with unfavorable change-of-control provisions
Define ERP cutover strategy (big-bang vs. phased) and confirm go-live date
Execute data extraction, cleanse, and validation for all modules entering acquirer ERP
Conduct user acceptance testing for all finance workflows in acquirer ERP environment
Execute production cutover and decommission legacy ERP under TSA exit timeline
Map target COA to acquirer COA structure (ERP configuration-layer for system cutover)
Configure COA mapping rules in ERP and validate against first post-close trial balance
Execute corporate credit card, P-card, and fleet card transition to acquirer programs
Map target SOX controls to acquirer framework
Determine SOX scope for acquired entities (in-scope vs. out)
items.mustHaveReason: SOX scoping determination for acquired entities must be completed — delays impact financial statement audit timeline and SEC filing deadlines.

items.description: Identify gaps in target control environment
Develop remediation plan for identified control gaps
Assess IT General Controls for acquired systems
items.mustHaveReason: ITGC gap assessment is required before first audited close. Unaddressed gaps create material weakness risk for the combined entity.

items.description: Confirm materiality thresholds for SOX scope inclusion of acquired entities
Align with external auditors on SOX integration timeline and testing expectations
Document significant accounts and processes for acquired entity SOX scope
Obtain management sign-off on final SOX scope boundary for combined entity
Execute design effectiveness assessment for all in-scope acquired controls
Execute operating effectiveness testing for priority-1 acquired controls
Document all control deficiencies and classify as significant deficiency or material weakness
Report control deficiencies to Audit Committee with remediation timeline
Evaluate control design adequacy for acquired revenue recognition processes
Review and rationalize duplicate controls between acquirer and target frameworks
Design new integrated controls for procure-to-pay and order-to-cash cycles
Update risk and control matrices (RCMs) to reflect combined entity control environment
Assign control remediation owners for all identified gaps
Establish remediation tracking dashboard and monthly reporting cadence
Complete remediation of all critical control gaps prior to year-end audit
Validate remediation effectiveness through re-testing of remediated controls
Inventory all in-scope applications and infrastructure supporting financial reporting
Assess logical access controls for all financially significant systems
Review change management controls for financially relevant systems
Test backup and recovery controls for all in-scope financial applications
Remediate ITGC deficiencies and validate with external auditors before year-end
Conduct fraud risk assessment for combined entity as required by AS 2401
Update whistleblower and fraud reporting hotline to cover acquired employees
Review antifraud controls in journal entry and account reconciliation processes
Conduct anti-bribery and FCPA risk assessment for cross-border acquired operations
Assess tone-at-the-top and control environment maturity for acquired entity
Review target's code of conduct, ethics policies, and compliance training programs
Align management's risk assessment process to acquirer COSO framework
Integrate monitoring activities and control self-assessment programs
Assess acquired entity's disclosure controls and procedures (DC&P) under Rule 13a-15
Update sub-certification process to include acquired entity management signatories
Confirm CEO/CFO certifications under Sections 302 and 906 reflect combined entity scope
Review disclosure committee charter and expand membership to cover acquired operations
Brief Audit Committee on integration impact to internal controls scope and timeline
Establish regular internal controls integration update cadence with Audit Committee
Present year-end ICFR management assessment conclusions to Audit Committee
Develop combined management testing plan covering all in-scope controls
Execute management testing of key controls under integrated RCM framework
Finalize management's assessment of ICFR effectiveness for combined entity annual report
Review deal structure for tax efficiency (§338, §336 elections)
File all required federal and state tax change-of-ownership notifications
Confirm tax return filing obligations for stub period
Document intercompany transfer pricing policies
Assess Pillar Two (Global Minimum Tax) exposure by jurisdiction
File CFIUS voluntary notice if applicable
File merger clearance notifications in all required jurisdictions
Identify and value NOLs, credits and other tax attributes
Evaluate §382 limitations on NOL utilization resulting from ownership change
Confirm tax basis step-up elections and purchase price allocation methodology
Review holding company structure post-close for tax efficiency and repatriation planning
Prepare and file stub period federal consolidated income tax return
File all required state and local income tax returns for stub period
Establish unified federal and state tax compliance calendar for combined entity
Review and rationalize tax compliance software and e-filing platforms
Conduct economic analysis to support arm's-length pricing for new intercompany transactions
Prepare or update master file and local file documentation under OECD BEPS Action 13
File Country-by-Country Report (CbCR) for applicable fiscal year post-close
Assess need for Advance Pricing Agreements (APAs) in high-risk jurisdictions
Model GloBE effective tax rate by constituent entity for Pillar Two liability quantification
Identify qualified domestic minimum top-up tax (QDMTT) elections available in each jurisdiction
Establish Pillar Two data collection process and integrate into tax provision workflow
Monitor CFIUS mitigation agreement conditions and establish compliance reporting
Track and respond to all competition authority information requests post-filing
File export control and ITAR notifications for any acquired defense or dual-use technology
Model §382 annual limitation and projected utilization schedule for acquired NOLs
Identify and preserve acquired R&D tax credits and employment tax incentives
Assess impact of post-close restructuring on §382 and tax attribute preservation
Conduct nexus review for combined entity in all states following ownership change
Assess state income and franchise tax combined reporting obligations
Review property tax assessments and exemptions for acquired real and personal property
Confirm sales and use tax registration obligations in all new nexus states
Assess VAT/GST registration and filing obligations for cross-border acquired operations
Review customs duty and import/export classification for acquired supply chain transactions
Assess target's tax software suite and determine integration or consolidation path
Integrate target ERP tax configuration into acquirer system or establish bridge solution
Prepare opening balance sheet deferred tax asset and liability schedule for purchase accounting
Integrate acquired entity into acquirer's ASC 740 tax provision process for first combined period
Establish cash pooling and sweeping arrangements
Review target debt instruments and covenant compliance
Notify all banks of ownership change and update KYC/AML documentation
Assess FX exposure across all transaction currencies
Review and update credit facility documentation post-close
Consolidate target bank accounts and eliminate redundant cash accounts
Integrate target into acquirer cash visibility and daily reporting platform
Review and rationalize short-term investment policy for combined entity liquidity reserves
Consolidate banking panel and negotiate combined relationship pricing and fees
Update bank mandates and authorized signatory lists for all retained accounts
Complete KYC/AML documentation refresh for all cross-border banking relationships
Develop combined FX risk management policy and hedge accounting designation strategy
Execute netting and natural hedge review to reduce gross FX exposures
Assess hedge accounting eligibility under ASC 815 / IFRS 9 for acquired hedging instruments
Obtain lender consent or waiver for change-of-control provisions in existing facilities
Model combined entity leverage and coverage ratios against covenant thresholds
Assess opportunity to refinance or consolidate credit facilities for better pricing
Inventory all target debt instruments including bonds, term loans, and revolver draws
Determine retirement, assumption, or refinancing strategy for each acquired debt instrument
Calculate and disclose fair value adjustments for acquired debt in purchase price allocation
Inventory all target insurance policies and assess coverage adequacy for combined entity
Obtain D&O, E&O, and cyber liability tail coverage for pre-close acquired entity period
Consolidate property and casualty insurance programs and eliminate duplicate coverages
Inventory all parent guarantees, letters of credit, and surety bonds issued by or for acquired entity
Determine which acquired guarantees require novation, replacement, or continuation
Update treasury investment policy to reflect combined entity risk tolerance and liquidity needs
Review counterparty credit limits and approved instruments for short-term investments
Integrate acquired entity into acquirer 13-week cash flow forecast model
Develop long-range (12-month) cash forecast for combined entity and present to CFO
Establish variance analysis process comparing actual cash to forecast with root cause commentary
Integrate acquired entity payment runs into acquirer payment factory or ERP payment module
Review and consolidate ACH, wire, and card payment approval workflows and controls
Coordinate debt payoff wire instructions and execute refinancing draws at close
Integrate target budget into acquirer planning cycle
Establish cost and revenue synergy baseline and tracking model
Assign synergy owners and reporting cadence
Prepare first combined P&L and cash flow forecast
Define combined entity KPI library aligned to strategic objectives
Establish KPI ownership, data sources, and calculation methodology for each metric
Baseline KPI performance for both entities pre-close and set Day 100 targets
Design integrated management reporting pack covering combined entity KPIs and flash reporting cadence
Automate monthly management reporting data extraction from combined ERP systems
Prepare first combined Board financial reporting package post-close
Align Board reporting cadence, format, and commentary standards across combined entity
Build synergy realization waterfall model with run-rate vs. one-time split
Report synergy actuals vs. case quarterly to SteerCo with variance commentary
Document target standalone cost structure prior to integration adjustments
Identify stranded and dis-synergy costs embedded in standalone cost base
Establish combined entity revenue baseline segmented by product, region, and channel
Identify revenue at risk from customer overlap, churn, or channel conflict post-close
Baseline net working capital for combined entity and compare to deal model assumptions
Identify working capital optimization opportunities (DSO, DPO, DIO) in combined entity
Develop three-to-five year long-range financial plan for combined entity
Validate long-range plan assumptions against deal model and present to CEO and CFO
Build upside, base, and downside scenario models for combined entity financials
Stress-test integration synergy case under macro and execution risk scenarios
Prepare updated investor financial guidance reflecting combined entity pro forma outlook
Coordinate FP&A inputs for earnings call and investor presentations post-close
Develop combined entity financial fact sheet and key metrics summary for investor community
Integrate target entity into acquirer annual budgeting and re-forecasting process
Establish rolling forecast cadence (quarterly or monthly) for combined entity with zero-based assumptions review
Assess GDPR/CCPA compliance for all personal data processing
Initiate Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) for EU operations
Update privacy notices and cookie policies for all websites
Conduct cybersecurity risk assessment of target environment
Review and update incident response plan for combined entity
Identify AI systems in scope and assess EU AI Act compliance
Conduct network architecture review of target environment and identify segmentation gaps
Implement network access controls and firewall rule review for interconnected environments
Establish network monitoring and intrusion detection coverage across combined entity
Inventory all endpoint devices in target environment and assess EDR coverage gaps
Deploy acquirer-standard endpoint detection and response (EDR) tooling to target devices
Assess cloud infrastructure security posture (AWS, Azure, GCP) for target environment
Enforce cloud security baseline policies (CSPM, CWPP) across combined cloud estate
Review and remediate privileged access and misconfigured storage buckets in target cloud
items.mustHaveReason: Privileged access remediation in target cloud is a critical security item. Unremediated access exposes the combined entity to breach and insider threat from close.

items.description: Inventory target's critical third-party and vendor relationships and assess cyber risk
Apply acquirer third-party risk management (TPRM) framework to target vendor portfolio
Enroll all target employees in acquirer security awareness training program
Run phishing simulation for combined workforce and report results to CISO
Apply data classification taxonomy to target data assets (public, internal, confidential, restricted)
Implement data loss prevention (DLP) controls aligned to combined entity classification policy
Commission external penetration test of target external attack surface at close
Remediate critical and high findings from penetration test within agreed SLA
Onboard target environment into acquirer vulnerability scanning platform
Establish patching cadence and SLA for combined entity critical and high CVEs
Review target privileged access accounts and enforce least-privilege principles
Integrate target identity infrastructure into acquirer IAM / SSO platform
Enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) across all target user accounts
Review target business continuity and disaster recovery (BCP/DR) plans for cyber scenarios
Integrate target into combined entity BCP/DR testing and tabletop exercise program
Assess target backup and recovery posture for resilience against ransomware attack
Validate immutable backup capability and test recovery time objectives for critical systems
Map breach notification obligations across all jurisdictions of combined entity operation
Establish documented breach notification runbook with DPA and legal escalation paths
Conduct tabletop exercise simulating data breach scenario across combined entity teams
Obtain cyber insurance coverage review and update policy limits for combined entity risk profile
Report combined entity cybersecurity risk posture and remediation roadmap to Board
Establish ESG baseline metrics for combined entity
Assess CSRD/SEC climate disclosure obligations for combined entity
Integrate target into acquirer carbon accounting framework
Quantify combined entity Scope 1 and 2 greenhouse gas emissions using GHG Protocol methodology
Map material Scope 3 emission categories for combined entity and prioritize data collection
Set combined entity near-term and net-zero emissions reduction targets aligned to SBTi
Assess target supplier ESG risk profile against acquirer supply chain sustainability standards
Integrate target procurement data into acquirer supplier ESG scoring and monitoring platform
Compile combined entity workforce diversity metrics (gender, ethnicity, seniority) for baseline reporting
Align DEI reporting definitions, methodologies, and targets across combined entity
Conduct human rights due diligence review across combined entity operations and supply chain
Assess target's modern slavery and forced labour policy against acquirer standards and applicable law
Baseline combined entity water withdrawal and consumption by facility and region
Baseline combined entity waste generation, recycling rate, and landfill diversion metrics
Map material ESG stakeholder groups for combined entity and assess engagement requirements
Develop combined entity ESG stakeholder engagement plan and materiality assessment roadmap
Establish combined entity ESG governance structure including Board oversight and management committee
Align ESG policies, codes of conduct, and ethics frameworks across combined entity
Assess eligibility of combined entity operations for green bond or sustainability-linked loan frameworks
Review existing target green financing instruments for change-of-control covenant compliance
Assess combined entity revenue and capex against EU Taxonomy technical screening criteria
Assess combined entity community investment, social programmes, and social impact KPIs
Stand up Integration Management Office (IMO) structure
items.mustHaveReason: IMO governance structure must be stood up at close. Without this item there is no program management — all other integration work is uncoordinated.

items.description: Assign workstream leads across all functional areas
items.mustHaveReason: Workstream lead assignments are a Day 1 prerequisite for accountability. No RACI = no ownership = cascading delays across all workstreams.

items.description: Finalize integration budget by workstream and phase
Establish weekly SteerCo reporting rhythm and template
Produce first integrated status report for executive leadership
Track integration spend vs. budget monthly
Establish integration risk register and assign risk owners by workstream
items.mustHaveReason: Integration risk register establishes the gating view of all program risks. Without this item the IMO is blind to blocking issues from Day 1.

items.description: Define risk scoring methodology (likelihood × impact) for integration risks
Review and update integration risk register bi-weekly through Day 90
Define issue escalation process and SLA for resolution by severity tier
Maintain active issues log with owners, due dates, and resolution status
Conduct weekly issue resolution review with workstream leads
Develop organizational change management strategy and stakeholder impact assessment
Identify change champions across target and acquirer organizations
Deliver change readiness assessment survey at Day 30 and Day 90
Develop integrated communication plan covering employees, customers, and investors
Execute Day 1 employee announcement and FAQ distribution
Establish integration intranet page or portal for ongoing updates
Build combined stakeholder map identifying key influencers, decision-makers, and at-risk personnel
Assign executive sponsors to each major stakeholder group
Establish clean room protocol for pre-close information sharing with legal oversight
Define data classification tiers and permissible uses within clean room environment
Complete Day 1 readiness checklist sign-off across all workstreams prior to close
Conduct Day 1 war-room walkthrough with workstream leads and IT
Document integration synergy targets by category (cost, revenue, capital)
Assign synergy owners and establish monthly synergy tracking cadence
Report realized synergies vs. targets in SteerCo deck beginning Day 30
Define integration KPIs and success metrics for each workstream
Publish integration scorecard dashboard for leadership review at Day 30 and Day 60
Conduct mid-integration retrospective at Day 60 with workstream leads
Document lessons learned and update integration playbook for future deals
Define multi-tier escalation matrix from workstream to SteerCo to Board
Hold first SteerCo governance meeting within 5 business days of close
Assess internal integration resource capacity gaps and determine third-party advisor needs
Onboard integration management system or project tracking tool for all workstreams
Inventory all target real estate leases and assess consolidation opportunities
Identify lease assignments requiring landlord consent
Assess co-location and office consolidation opportunities
Audit physical security systems (access control, CCTV, alarms) at all target locations
Align target physical security protocols to acquirer enterprise security standards
Revoke target legacy access credentials and issue acquirer-standard badges at close
Update mailing addresses and redirect mail for all acquired entity locations
Develop signage transition plan and timeline for rebranding all acquired locations
Review environmental compliance obligations and certificates of occupancy for all acquired sites
Develop headcount-driven space utilization model for consolidated real estate footprint
Inventory in-flight capital improvement projects at acquired facilities and assess continuation
Identify leases approaching renewal and negotiate terms leveraging combined portfolio scale
Identify excess space candidates for subleasing and engage broker where consolidation eliminates need
Map target payroll processes to acquirer payroll platform and confirm Day 1 continuity
items.mustHaveReason: Payroll continuity plan must be confirmed before Day 1 — payroll failure at close is a critical employee relations event that triggers immediate attrition risk.

items.description: Conduct cultural assessment survey across both organizations and identify integration friction points
Identify key talent at risk of attrition and implement retention agreements or incentive plans
Define HRIS consolidation roadmap and migrate acquired employee records to acquirer system
Confirm employee benefit continuity at close (COBRA, 401k, health)
items.mustHaveReason: Employee benefit continuity (COBRA, 401k, health) must be confirmed before Day 1. Lapses create immediate legal exposure and employee relations crises.

items.description: Execute W-2 and payroll tax cutover for stub period (EIN transition, FICA, withholding)
Manage benefits enrollment timing — open enrollment windows, qualifying life events, COBRA
Transfer or establish workers compensation policies and experience modifier ratings
Communicate equity award treatment to acquired employees (vesting, conversion ratios)
Identify all vendor contracts requiring novation and obtain counterparty consent or execute novation agreements pre-close
items.mustHaveReason: Contract novation identification must begin at close. Missed novation deadlines expose the acquirer to contract termination and counterparty disputes.

items.description: Identify all customer contracts requiring novation, prioritize by revenue concentration, and execute novation or assignment with customer consent
Execute novation or assignment of all real-property leases and confirm landlord consent obtained prior to or at close
Process government contract novation under FAR 42.12 — file novation agreement with cognizant contracting officer and obtain written recognition before performance resumes
Record patent assignment and trademark transfer with USPTO (and foreign registries as applicable); update ownership records and docketing system
Execute trade secret and know-how transfer documentation, including inventory of confidential materials, access controls handover, and chain-of-custody records
Adopt post-close board resolutions for acquired entity covering officer appointments, banking authorizations, and ratification of transaction documents
items.mustHaveReason: Post-close board resolutions are required to establish legal authority for the acquired entity. Without them, banking authorizations and officer appointments are invalid at Day 1.

items.description: File articles of merger, UCC-3 termination/assignment statements, and updated registered agent designation with each applicable state secretary of state
Review and tag all contracts in the data room for change-of-control, anti-assignment, and consent-required provisions; produce master consent tracker
Obtain required third-party consents on material contracts before contractual or regulatory deadlines and confirm in writing; escalate refusals to deal team
Map all non-compete and non-solicitation agreements covering key employees and former owners; confirm enforceability under applicable state law and record expiration dates
Identify all federal and state regulatory licenses (e.g., professional, financial, environmental) held by acquired entity and initiate transfer or re-issuance applications pre-close
items.mustHaveReason: Regulatory license transfers have statutory deadlines from close date. Failure to file creates gaps in operating authority — the acquired entity may not be legally permitted to operate.

items.description: Transfer or renew all local business permits, municipal licenses, and zoning approvals; confirm no gap in operating authority at Day 1
Review all assumed product and service warranty obligations; establish reserve methodology and integrate with acquirer warranty management process
Build indemnification obligation register from SPA schedules and known litigation; assign ownership to legal/finance and set calendar reminders for indemnification deadlines
Update power of attorney designations for acquired legal entity to reflect new ownership and authorized signatories effective at close
Establish R&W insurance claims reporting process, map discovery-to-notice timeline against policy window, and assign internal claims coordinator
Document escrow release schedule and all conditions precedent (earn-out milestones, rep survival periods, indemnity baskets); track in deal mechanics register with responsible owner
Plan and execute Day 1 employee town hall / all-hands meeting including leadership Q&A, integration vision, and role clarity messaging
Develop and maintain internal FAQ document covering compensation, benefits, reporting lines, and integration timeline — update as answers evolve post-close
Establish ongoing integration newsletter and employee communications cadence (bi-weekly or monthly) covering milestones, workstream updates, and leadership messages through Year 1
Draft and distribute formal customer change-of-control notification letters per contract requirements and applicable law, prioritized by revenue concentration
Draft and send vendor and supplier change-of-control notification letters and confirm assignment consent where required by contract before Day 1 performance obligations resume
Develop press release and media strategy for close announcement including spokesperson briefing, embargo management, journalist Q&A, and social amplification plan
Notify partner and channel organizations of transaction close, distribute enablement materials, and confirm go-forward commercial arrangements and program terms
Make and document brand architecture decision (co-brand vs. full rebrand vs. endorsed brand) with executive sign-off and publish internal brand transition guidelines and timelines
Execute Day 1 website updates for corporate messaging, about page, leadership bios, and careers section to reflect the combined entity and transaction close
Manage social media account transition including handle changes, follower migration strategy, and coordinated Day 1 announcement posts across LinkedIn, X, and other active platforms
Prepare investor and analyst Day 1 talking points covering deal rationale, integration strategy, synergy targets, and financial guidance implications; brief IR team before market open

Identify and file required notifications to industry-specific regulatory bodies (e.g., FINRA, FCC, state insurance commissioners, banking regulators) triggered by change of control within mandated deadlines
Assess target IT strategic alignment with acquirer technology roadmap
Map IT decision rights and governance structure for combined entity
Harmonize IT governance frameworks (GRC platforms, risk management)
Consolidate IT policies, standards, and acceptable use policies
Establish combined IT risk register and reporting cadence
Assess target IT org structure and identify leadership alignment
Define Day 1 IT reporting structure and interim leadership roles
Identify critical IT talent and design retention incentive plan
Assess contractor and vendor staffing dependencies in target IT
Inventory all ERP, CRM, and HRIS systems across target and acquirer
Develop ERP migration or consolidation strategy (target to acquirer platform)
Map chart of accounts and master data between ERP systems
Assess industry-specific platform overlap (PSA, billing, payment processing)
Plan email, calendar, and collaboration tool migration (M365/Google)
items.mustHaveReason: Email, calendar, and collaboration tool migration plan is a Day 1 operational dependency. Without it, the acquired workforce loses communication and productivity from close.

items.description: Consolidate document management systems and shared drives
Inventory RPA, iPaaS, and workflow automation tools for rationalization
Inventory all cloud services (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS) across both entities
items.mustHaveReason: Cloud services inventory is the foundational infrastructure baseline. All IT cost rationalization, security, and DR planning depends on this item.

items.description: Develop cloud consolidation strategy and FinOps cost model
Assess on-premises server, network, and data center consolidation opportunities
Consolidate help desk and ITSM tool platforms
Integrate disaster recovery and business continuity plans for combined entity
Assess data architecture and establish combined data governance framework
Plan master data management (MDM) consolidation across entities
Rationalize BI and analytics platforms; plan unified reporting layer
Establish data retention, archival, and destruction policies for combined entity
Assess target security posture and compliance certifications (SOC 2, ISO 27001)
Identify regulatory compliance gaps (PCI-DSS, HIPAA, licensing requirements)
Integrate SOC operations and consolidate SIEM/monitoring platforms
Consolidate IAM platforms and implement unified access provisioning
Review data loss prevention (DLP) and encryption standards across entities
Assess user provisioning and deprovisioning processes across target systems
Review segregation of duties (SoD) in financial systems and resolve conflicts
Harmonize change management and release processes (CI/CD, CAB)
Review batch processing, job scheduling, and backup/recovery controls
Align software development lifecycle and QA testing standards
Consolidate vendor inventories and assess third-party risk exposure
Identify vendor concentration risks in critical IT services
Review IT vendor contracts for change-of-control clauses and renegotiation opportunities
Assess MSP/MSSP and outsourcing contracts for consolidation or transition
Inventory client-facing digital products, portals, and API ecosystem
Assess product engineering capabilities and platform architecture alignment
Review client data handling, consent management, and privacy compliance
Analyze combined IT spend as % of revenue and benchmark against industry
Audit all software licenses and SaaS subscriptions for consolidation savings
Identify and quantify IT integration synergies (infrastructure, licensing, headcount)
Estimate IT integration costs by workstream and build migration budget
Define sales rules of engagement: territory ownership, account boundaries, and dual-coverage resolution protocol effective Day 1
items.mustHaveReason: Sales Rules of Engagement must be defined on Day 1 — without them, duplicate customer outreach, channel conflict, and lost deals occur immediately. Programs without this item average 12% lower revenue retention in Year 1.

items.description: Develop and distribute customer-facing Day 1 communication plan covering ownership continuity, point-of-contact changes, and transaction rationale
Map all shared and overlapping accounts across both entities; assign unified account owners and resolve channel conflict before first joint sales motion
items.mustHaveReason: Account overlap mapping prevents dual-coverage conflicts and defines territory ownership. Unresolved overlaps trigger attrition of both sales teams within 60 days of close.

items.description: Implement pipeline freeze protocol for deals-in-flight: freeze duplicate pipeline entries, define single system of record, and align CRM stage definitions
Build combined sales force capacity model: reps by segment, quota capacity, ramp-adjusted capacity, and coverage ratios for Year 1 plan
Notify channel partners and resellers of transaction close; confirm go-forward program terms, MDF allocations, and partner tier assignments
Consolidate competitive intelligence databases and sales battlecards across both entities into a unified competitive readiness kit
Review all material customer contracts for price escalators, MFN clauses, volume commitments, and renewal provisions; flag risk items for legal review
Perform cross-sell and whitespace analysis: identify accounts where combined portfolio unlocks net-new revenue opportunities above baseline
Define combined revenue attribution model: bookings definitions, ARR/TCV recognition, and quota credit rules for cross-sell and bundled deals
Conduct structured win/loss knowledge transfer sessions between sales teams to preserve institutional deal-cycle knowledge and customer context
Align pricing and packaging strategy across combined portfolio: rationalize SKUs, resolve price-point conflicts, and publish unified price book by Day 60
items.mustHaveReason: Pricing and packaging misalignment between entities creates customer confusion, margin erosion, and channel partner defection. A unified strategy must be defined by Day 60 to protect first-year revenue.

items.description: Integrate CPQ (configure-price-quote) systems or define bridging process to ensure accurate combined-product quoting before CPQ consolidation completes
Align sales incentive compensation plans (SPIFs, accelerators, clawbacks) and confirm Day 1 pay continuation for acquired sales staff
Set Year 1 quotas for combined sales team using unified capacity model; communicate before end of Day 60 to preserve rep motivation through integration
Execute brand architecture decision (co-brand / full rebrand / endorsed brand) with executive sign-off; publish internal brand transition guidelines and milestone timeline
items.mustHaveReason: Brand architecture decision (co-brand vs. rebrand vs. endorsed) is a gating item for all marketing, communications, and digital workstreams. Delayed decision creates cascading rework across 12+ workstreams.

items.description: Transfer demand generation programs, lead routing rules, and campaign calendars to acquirer marketing operations; ensure no coverage gap at Day 1
Rationalize marketing technology stack (MAPs, ABM platforms, analytics tools) and define consolidated martech architecture for Year 1
Transfer content libraries, asset repositories, and brand collateral to acquirer DAM system; audit for brand compliance and PII removal
Consolidate event and conference calendars to prevent conflicting presence at same venue; align sponsorship budgets and speaker submissions
Migrate digital advertising accounts (Google Ads, LinkedIn, paid social) to acquirer billing entity; preserve campaign history and audience data
Define SEO and domain consolidation strategy; implement redirects, preserve domain authority, and align URL structure under combined web presence
Harmonize customer SLAs across acquired and acquirer contracts; identify SLA conflicts and define Day 1 unified service commitment to avoid breach exposure
items.mustHaveReason: SLA harmonization across customer contracts prevents breach-of-contract risk from Day 1. Conflicting SLAs between entities expose the acquirer to immediate penalty and churn.

items.description: Map all renewal dates across combined customer base; assign owners and establish renewal calendar to protect ARR from Day 1
Unify customer health score methodology and populate combined CS platform with acquired customer health data before first QBR cycle
Consolidate CS tooling (Gainsight, ChurnZero, Totango) and define single platform for combined team; migrate customer data before acquired team transitions
Establish combined net revenue retention baseline and churn cohort analysis as the primary CS health metric for Year 1 integration reporting
Assess ITAR/EAR export control obligations for acquired products, technologies, and customer base; confirm all necessary licenses and agreements are in place at close
items.mustHaveReason: ITAR/EAR trade compliance must be assessed on Day 1 for any target with dual-use or defense-relevant products. Violation of export control regulations triggers criminal liability — there is no cure period.

items.description: Build consolidated supplier inventory across both entities; tier suppliers by spend concentration and criticality to production continuity
Audit target supplier quality management system (QMS/ISO 9001) against acquirer standards; identify gap suppliers and initiate corrective action plans
items.mustHaveReason: Supplier quality management system gaps expose combined entity to product liability, regulatory recall risk, and customer contract defaults. Audit must begin by Day 60 to meet supply chain assurance timelines.

items.description: Review contract manufacturing agreements for change-of-control provisions, exclusivity clauses, and minimum volume commitments; renegotiate where consolidated volumes improve terms
Assess 3PL and logistics provider overlap; identify consolidation opportunities and renegotiate freight rates using combined shipping volume leverage
Review combined warehouse and distribution center footprint for consolidation, repurposing, or exit opportunities; assess proximity to combined customer base
Align inventory valuation methodologies (FIFO/LIFO/WAC) across entities and quantify opening balance sheet inventory adjustment required at close
Harmonize bill of materials structures and part numbering conventions across product families; enable combined sourcing and consolidated MRP runs
Develop SKU rationalization plan to eliminate duplicative products, reduce carrying costs, and simplify combined product catalog for channel and ERP consolidation
Evaluate combined plant and manufacturing footprint for consolidation, capacity rebalancing, and make-vs-buy decisions; develop Year 1 rationalization roadmap
items.mustHaveReason: Plant and manufacturing footprint rationalization is the highest-value supply chain synergy lever. Delaying this assessment past Year 1 forfeits the primary capex and stranded-cost savings opportunity.

items.description: Integrate ERP supply chain modules (MRP, WMS, TMS) across entities; define interim bridging process until full ERP consolidation completes
Inventory and classify all acquired IP: patents, trademarks, trade secrets, source code, and proprietary algorithms; confirm chain of title at close
Transfer source code repositories to acquirer VCS (Git/GitHub/ADO); confirm access controls, branch protection, and CI/CD pipeline continuity at Day 1
Integrate patent portfolio into acquirer IP docketing system; identify prosecution deadlines, maintenance fee calendars, and cross-licensing obligations
Conduct technical debt assessment across acquired codebase; quantify remediation cost and incorporate into Year 1 engineering budget and roadmap
Define combined engineering org structure and reporting lines; assign technical leads for each product area and communicate to engineering teams by Day 30
Merge development environments, CI/CD pipelines, and test infrastructure into unified platform; maintain parallel environments until cutover is validated
Harmonize product roadmaps across entities: identify overlap, gaps, and sequencing conflicts; publish combined roadmap with stakeholder sign-off by Day 60
items.mustHaveReason: Product roadmap conflict — competing features, overlapping SKUs, and misaligned engineering priorities — is the primary cause of post-merger R&D attrition. A harmonized roadmap must exist by Day 60 or key product talent exits.

items.description: Identify overlapping product features and develop sunset plan for duplicate capabilities; communicate deprecation timelines to affected customers
Design combined API and integration layer architecture to enable bundled product delivery and partner ecosystem connectivity across combined portfolio
Develop R&D spend allocation model for combined portfolio: define investment thesis, funding tiers by product line, and headcount allocation methodology
Migrate clinical trial data under FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2) requirements; confirm IRB notification, chain-of-custody documentation, and sponsor transfer filings
items.mustHaveReason: Clinical trial data migration is subject to FDA 21 CFR Part 11 / ICH E6 requirements and IRB obligations. Data loss or chain-of-custody break is irreversible and can invalidate ongoing trials.

items.description: File statutory works council and union notification in all jurisdictions requiring pre-close consultation (EU, UK, Germany, France, Netherlands); obtain required opinions before sign-off
items.mustHaveReason: Works council and union notification deadlines are statutory in EU, UK, and APAC jurisdictions — failure triggers injunctions that can halt close. Pre-close notification is non-negotiable for any cross-border deal.

items.description: Review all collective bargaining agreements covering acquired employees; identify successorship obligations, re-bargaining triggers, and wage scale integration requirements
Announce N-1 leadership appointments on Day 1: direct reports to CEO/CHRO, workstream leads, and functional heads; confirm titles, compensation, and reporting lines before close
items.mustHaveReason: N-1 leadership clarity on Day 1 is the single largest driver of retention and integration velocity. Org ambiguity at the leadership level triggers departure cascades within 90 days.

items.description: Complete position-level mapping across combined org: align job titles, grades, FLSA classifications, and identify role eliminations, splits, and new hires required for go-forward model
Align severance and separation policies across entities; confirm which employees are covered under target severance plans and communicate clearly to reduce ambiguity
Review all non-compete and non-solicitation agreements covering acquired employees and former owners; assess enforceability by jurisdiction and flag agreements protecting key customer relationships
Complete EEO/OFCCP affirmative action program transfer for federal contractors; file updated written affirmative action plans within required timeframes
Determine background check re-screening requirements for acquired employees under acquirer's policy; execute re-screening where required before system access is granted
Deploy acquirer onboarding program for acquired employees: I-9 re-verification where required, handbook acknowledgment, system access provisioning, and Day 1 orientation
Develop ERISA plan consolidation roadmap: pension, 401(k), and welfare benefit plan mergers, spin-offs, and terminations; file required IRS/PBGC notices within statutory deadlines

Initiate visa and work permit transfers for acquired employees on employer-sponsored status (H-1B, L-1, Tier 2 UK, EU Blue Card); file amended petitions with USCIS/UKVI/authorities within statutory grace periods
items.mustHaveReason: Immigration and visa transfers for acquired employees on sponsored visas must be initiated immediately at close. Lapsed status triggers immediate removal from assigned work and potential legal liability.

items.description: Confirm HSR Act filing, waiting period expiration, and clearance from all applicable antitrust authorities (DOJ, FTC, EC, CMA, others) before close; respond to Second Requests within statutory deadlines
items.mustHaveReason: HSR/antitrust filing deadlines are statutory and non-waivable — missing the waiting period or Second Request response creates deal invalidation risk. This item must be cleared before close.

items.description: Complete CFIUS national security review and obtain clearance or mitigation agreement for any transaction involving a foreign acquirer or assets with national security implications
File and obtain approval under applicable foreign investment review regimes (FIRMMA, ICA Canada, FIRB Australia, EU screening mechanisms) before close
Review all consent decrees, settlement agreements, and regulatory orders binding the acquired entity; assess applicability to acquirer post-close and notify relevant authorities as required
Conduct environmental liability assessment for all acquired operating sites: Phase I/II reviews, Superfund/CERCLA exposure, permit transfers, and remediation obligations
Compile product liability register from SPA schedules, known claims, and regulatory recall history; set litigation reserves and assign internal claims management ownership
Consolidate commercial insurance programs (GL, D&O, E&O, cyber, property) under acquirer's broker; confirm no coverage gap and update named insured designations at close
Review government and defense contract compliance obligations: FAR/DFARS flow-downs, CAS compliance, security clearance continuity, and novation requirements under FAR 42.12
Transfer all active litigation files, regulatory investigations, and arbitration proceedings to acquirer legal team; confirm counsel of record assignments and litigation reserve adequacy
items.mustHaveReason: Ongoing litigation inventory and reserve handover must be complete at Day 1. Unrecorded litigation exposure becomes the acquirer's liability at close — post-close discovery of material suits triggers restatement and indemnity disputes.

items.description: Align litigation reserve methodology across entities and submit combined reserve schedule to CFO and external auditors within first-close reporting window
Confirm D&O tail insurance coverage for pre-close acts of acquired entity directors and officers; obtain certificates of insurance from target before final closing
Review all debt agreements for change-of-control provisions, mandatory prepayment triggers, and consent requirements; confirm no covenant default or cross-acceleration event at close
items.mustHaveReason: Debt covenant review must be completed at close — many credit agreements contain change-of-control provisions that trigger cross-default, mandatory repayment, or fee obligations. Failure to identify creates immediate liquidity events.

items.description: Develop bank account rationalization plan: identify accounts for consolidation or closure, update authorized signatories, and migrate to acquirer banking relationships on defined timeline
Establish earn-out and contingent consideration tracking register: milestone definitions, measurement periods, performance baselines, and dispute resolution process per SPA terms
items.mustHaveReason: Earn-out and contingent consideration tracking requires a dedicated register from Day 1. Missed earn-out milestones trigger disputes, litigation, and material financial misstatement.

items.description: Execute working capital true-up calculation and payment per SPA mechanism; resolve any disputed items through defined arbitration process within contractual deadline
Design combined cash pooling and netting structure across acquirer and acquired entities; optimize intercompany lending arrangements and reduce external borrowing costs
Review combined FX exposure across entity currencies and adjust hedging program to cover transactional and translational risk in the combined entity's currency profile
Inventory all in-flight capital projects at acquired entity: project name, scope, budget, stage of completion, owner, and lender or permit dependencies
Conduct status review of all in-flight projects with project managers; flag projects over budget, behind schedule, or with unresolved permit issues for immediate triage
Review construction and EPC contracts for change-of-control provisions, performance bond obligations, and contractor consent requirements; obtain consents or negotiate amendments
Transfer or re-apply for environmental permits, building permits, and zoning variances for in-flight construction projects; ensure no permit gap that halts active construction
Confirm insurance and bonding continuity for active construction projects; update named insured and loss payee designations on builders risk, general liability, and surety bonds
Obtain lender consents and waive change-of-control provisions in project financing agreements for all in-flight capital projects with third-party financing
Triage all inherited in-flight capex projects using stop / continue / accelerate framework; assign owners, confirm funding sources, and publish go-forward capital plan by Day 30
items.mustHaveReason: In-flight capex projects must be triaged by Day 30 — inherited projects with no owner, no budget alignment, or lapsed permits become stranded costs. Triage enables stop/continue decisions before sunk cost compounds.

items.description: Integrate acquired entity into acquirer capital allocation framework: approval thresholds, CAPEX request process, NPV hurdle rates, and multi-year capital plan
Stand up Separation Management Office (SMO): assign separation lead, define SMO governance model, and establish weekly separation operating rhythm from Day 1
Develop shared service separation plan by function: identify which shared services remain under TSA, which are being built standalone, and sequencing for each exit
Establish standalone cost baseline: model the full run-rate cost of operating as an independent entity and identify stranded cost elements requiring elimination or restructuring
Extract and migrate commingled data from shared systems before TSA termination: identify data by ownership, cleanse PII, and validate completeness in standalone environment
items.mustHaveReason: Commingled data extraction is irreversible — once TSA ends, access to shared systems is terminated. Failure to extract and migrate clean data sets before exit creates permanent data loss and regulatory exposure.

items.description: Complete IT infrastructure separation from seller's shared environment: stand up independent network, identity, and compute before TSA system access termination date
Complete legal entity separation: transfer assets, contracts, licenses, and employees to standalone entities; file all required state and regulatory notifications
Execute stranded cost elimination plan: restructure or eliminate costs that cannot exist in standalone entity; track progress monthly and report to SteerCo through Day 90

Assess data localization and sovereignty requirements for combined entity's data flows (GDPR, China DSL/PIPL, India DPDP, Russia 242-FZ); implement controls before Day 30 to prevent unlawful cross-border transfers
items.mustHaveReason: Data localization and sovereignty requirements (EU AI Act, GDPR, China DSL, India DPDP) prohibit certain cross-border data flows from the moment of close. Non-compliance triggers regulatory enforcement and interrupts operations.

items.description: Establish combined breach notification protocol: define incident detection, escalation chain, regulatory notification timelines (72-hr GDPR, US state laws), and customer notification process
Complete Privacy Impact Assessments (PIAs/DPIAs) for all high-risk data processing activities inherited through acquisition; document findings and mitigation plans per GDPR Art. 35
Map all VAT, GST, and indirect tax obligations arising from TSA services; confirm invoicing entity, tax treatment, and recovery mechanism before first TSA invoice cycle
items.mustHaveReason: TSA services are taxable supplies in most jurisdictions — VAT/GST/indirect tax mapping must be completed at Day 1 or TSA invoicing creates 15–25% unbudgeted tax leakage with no retroactive remedy.

items.description: Execute lockbox and cash sweep cutover: redirect customer remittances from seller's bank accounts to acquirer accounts and confirm treasury systems reflect correct entity routing from Day 1
items.mustHaveReason: Lockbox and cash sweep mechanics must be cut over at Day 1 — under TSA the acquiree's bank accounts remain active. Without this item, cash remittance loops to the wrong entity, triggering treasury reconciliation failures.

items.description: Implement logical data segregation within all shared TSA systems: enforce access controls, data tagging, and audit logging to prevent co-mingling of acquirer and seller data in joint-operated environments
items.mustHaveReason: Logical data segregation within shared TSA systems is required immediately at close. Without it, acquirer data and target data co-mingle in systems operated by the seller, creating privilege waiver and data sovereignty risk.

items.description: Establish legal employment boundaries during HR TSA period: define which entity is employer of record, agree on HR support scope with seller, and mitigate joint employer risk through documented service terms
items.mustHaveReason: Co-employment risk during an HR TSA period creates joint and several employer liability. Without defined legal employment boundaries, the acquirer inherits the seller's wage-and-hour, benefits, and discrimination exposure.

items.description: Execute payroll shadow run on standalone platform for minimum one full payroll cycle before TSA exit; validate gross-to-net calculations, deduction accuracy, and tax withholding before cutover
items.mustHaveReason: A payroll shadow run (parallel processing before TSA exit) is the only reliable validation that the standalone payroll platform can handle the acquired population. TSA exits without this step fail at a 3× higher rate.

items.description: Execute Order-to-Cash cutover: migrate billing entity, payment routing, and revenue recognition system before TSA system access termination; validate first invoice cycle on standalone O2C platform
items.mustHaveReason: Order-to-cash cutover during TSA is the highest-risk operational transition — incorrect billing entity, payment routing, or revenue recognition triggers immediate customer dispute and financial restatement.

items.description: Plan and communicate P2P blackout window during TSA procurement system handover: define no-order period, buffer stock levels, and approval workarounds to maintain supply continuity
items.mustHaveReason: P2P blackout window during TSA exit must be planned and communicated — unplanned procurement system downtime during TSA handover disrupts supply continuity and creates unauthorized commitment liability.

items.description: Execute TSA Data Processing Agreement / Addendum with seller before close: define data controller/processor roles, processing scope, security requirements, and sub-processor obligations under GDPR and applicable privacy law
items.mustHaveReason: A TSA Data Processing Agreement/Addendum is legally required under GDPR and most data privacy regulations before the seller can process personal data on behalf of the acquirer. Absence creates unlawful processing from Day 1.
